claims.claim: When ambient pressure decreases from 95 kPa to 20 kPa, the average safety valve open time (first venting) is shortened for 18650 cylindrical cells from 144 s to 133 s, for 10 Ah pouch cells from 475 s to 461 s, and for 15 Ah pouch cells from 559 s to 533 s.
claims.quote: As the pressure decreases from 95 kPa to 20 kPa, the average values of t1 (the average open time of the safety valve or the rupture time of the bag, which was counted by the video recorded in the tests) decrease from 144 s to 133 s, 475 s to 461 s, and 559 s to 533 s, for 18650 cylindrical cells, 10 Ah pouch cells, and 15 Ah pouch cells, respectively

claims.claim: When pressure decreases from 95 kPa to 20 kPa, the average TR trigger time (spark ejection time t2) is shortened for cylindrical cells (from 145 s to 133 s) but delayed for 10 Ah pouch cells (from 625 s to 643 s) and 15 Ah pouch cells (from 730 s to 761 s).
claims.quote: The average trigger time (t2) of spark ejections for cylindrical cells is 145 s in 95 kPa and 133 s in 20 kPa. However, t2 increases from 625 s to 643 s, and from 730 s to 761 s for 10 Ah and 15 Ah pouch cells, when the pressure decreases from 95 kPa to 20 kPa.

claims.claim: When ambient pressure decreases from 95 kPa to 20 kPa, the peak HRR decreases from 1.2 kW to 0.65 kW for a single cylindrical cell, from 14.2 kW to 1.2 kW for 5 cylindrical cells, from 26.3 kW to 6.1 kW for a 10 Ah pouch cell, and from 41.1 kW to 8.7 kW for a 15 Ah pouch cell.
claims.quote: The peak HRR decreases from 1.2 kW to 0.65 kW, 14.2 kW to 1.2 kW, 26.3 kW to 6.1 kW, and 41.1 kW to 8.7 kW for 1 cylindrical cell, 5 cylindrical cells, a 10 Ah pouch cell, and a 15 Ah pouch cell, respectively.

claims.claim: When pressure decreases from 95 kPa to 20 kPa, peak CO concentrations increase (e.g., from 0.21 vol.% to 0.91 vol.% for 5 cylindrical cells) and peak THC values increase (e.g., from 256 ppm to 3474 ppm for 5 cylindrical cells), while CO2 concentrations decrease, indicating an inverse relationship between thermal fire hazards and toxic/flammable gas emissions.
claims.quote: the average peak values of CO increase from 0.19 vol.% to 0.28 vol.%, 0.21 vol.% to 0.91 vol.%, 0.16 vol.% to 0.59 vol.%, and 0.24 vol.% to 0.72 vol.%, with the pressure drop from 95 kPa to 20 kPa. The average peak THC values in 20 kPa are about 1275 ppm, 3474 ppm, 819 ppm, and 1641 ppm for four groups of tests...which are higher than 1019 ppm, 256 ppm, 127 ppm, and 559 ppm under 95 kPa, respectively.

claims.claim: Pouch cells have higher peak HRR, THR, and thermal hazards of fire than cylindrical 18650 cells at both 95 kPa and 20 kPa, while cylindrical cells have higher concentrations of THC and CO per unit capacity than pouch cells, indicating a higher risk of toxic and potentially explosive gas emissions for cylindrical cells.
claims.quote: the values of peak HRR, THR, and peak CO per unit capacity for the pouch cell are higher than the values of the cylindrical cell, but the peak values of CO/CO2, THC and CO per capacity are lower than in cylindrical cells. It indicates that the pouch cell with a soft aluminum–polymer bag has higher thermal hazards of TR fire, but a lower concentration of toxic/combustible gases than cylindrical cells with hard cans.

claims.claim: LFP cells did not flame at 0% and 30% SOC, while LCO cells flamed at all SOC levels tested.
claims.quote: LFP tests did not flame at 0% and 30% SOCs, while LCO tests flamed at all SOCs. This is consistent with LFP cells having higher thermal stability compared to LCO cells.

claims.claim: OC was more abundant during cell venting, while EC and PO4(3-) were more abundant when flaming combustion occurred.
claims.quote: While OC was more abundant during cell venting, EC and PO4 3− were more abundant when flaming combustion occurred.

claims.claim: For LFP tests, the average EC fraction in PM2.5 increased from 0–7% at 0–50% SOC to 25–29% at 75% SOC.
claims.quote: The average EC fraction increased from 0–7% for 0–50% SOC to 25–29% for 75% SOC.

claims.claim: At LFP 50% and 75% SOC, the PO4(3-) fraction dominated PM2.5 mass at 34–44%, whereas it was practically absent at 0% and 30% SOC.
claims.quote: the OM fractions were much lower (1–20%), while the PO4 3− fraction, practically absent in the 0% and 30% SOC cases, dominated with 34–44% of PM2.5 mass.

claims.claim: Ignition of the emissions stream, rather than cell SOC, was identified as the primary determinant of the general chemical composition of emitted PM2.5 particles.
claims.quote: This indicates that ignition of the emissions stream, rather than cell SOC, is mostly responsible for the general composition of emitted PM2.5 particles.

claims.claim: For LFP tests, the anion-to-cation ratio was approximately 10:1 for venting-dominated tests and approximately 200:1 for tests with significant flaming, indicating strongly acidic freshly emitted particles.
claims.quote: For LFP tests, the anion/cation ratio ranges depended on combustion behavior, with ~10:1 for venting tests and ~200:1 for tests with significant flaming.

claims.claim: LCO tests had an increasing proportion of PM2.5 emissions from flaming combustion as SOC increased, and all LCO tests except one had more than 50% of PM2.5 emissions from flaming combustion.
claims.quote: LCO tests had an increasing proportion of emissions from flaming as SOC increased... All LCO tests except one had >50% of PM2.5 emissions come from flaming combustion.

claims.claim: Particles from LFP tests had higher organic matter (OM) but lower elemental carbon (EC) compared to particles from LCO tests, consistent with the higher thermal stability of LFP cells.
claims.quote: Overall, particles from LFP tests had higher OM but lower EC compared to LCO tests, consistent with the higher thermal stability of LFP cells.

claims.claim: The most abundant PM2.5 constituents from LIB thermal runaway combustion were OC, EC, PO4(3-), and fluoride, contributing 7–91%, 0.2–40%, 1–44%, and 0.7–3% to PM2.5 mass, respectively.
claims.quote: The most abundant PM2.5 constituents were OC, EC, 3−), and fluoride (F−), contributing 7–91%, 0.2–40%, 1–44%, and 0.7–3% to the PM2.5 phosphate (PO4 mass, respectively.

claims.claim: NH4+ abundance in PM2.5 was highest at low SOCs for both LFP and LCO cell types and decreased for mid-range SOCs, likely because NH4+ decomposes at high flaming temperatures.
claims.quote: NH4 + abundance was highest at low SOCs for both cell types and decreased for mid-range SOCs, especially in LCO tests, likely because NH4 + decomposes at high flaming temperatures.

claims.claim: Thermal runaway is characterised by rapid self-heating starting at around 150–220 °C and increasing to about 1000 °C.
claims.quote: thermal runaway is characterised by rapid self-heating starting at around 150–220 ◦C and increasing to about 1000 ◦C

claims.claim: The first step towards thermal runaway is commonly the breakdown of the SEI layer on the graphite anode, which occurs at about 90–120 °C.
claims.quote: the first step towards TR is commonly the breakdown of the SEI layer on the graphite anode, which occurs at about 90–120 ◦C

claims.claim: When the separator melts at temperatures in the range of 135–165 °C, electrical contact between the anode and cathode can result in an internal short circuit and self-discharge.
claims.quote: When the separator melts at temperatures in the range of 135–165 ◦C, the electrical contact between the anode and cathode can result in an ISC and, thereby, self-discharge.

claims.claim: As temperature reaches 200 °C and higher, all components of the electrolyte start to decompose exothermically, forming CO2, HF, hydrocarbons, and various fluorinated hydrocarbon gases.
claims.quote: As the temperature reaches 200 ◦C and higher, all components of the electrolyte start to decompose exothermically, upon which CO2, hydrogen fluoride (HF), hydrocarbons, and various fluorinated hydrocarbon gases are formed.

claims.claim: The cathode begins to decompose and release oxygen in the temperature range of 150–310 °C.
claims.quote: As the temperature increases further in the range of 150–310 ◦C, the cathode begins to decompose and release oxygen.

claims.claim: The total gas volume vented from LIB cells scaled linearly with cell capacity.
claims.quote: A clear trend found was that the gas volume scaled linearly with cell capacity

claims.claim: Reactive gases O2 and H2 are dissolved in the electrolyte to a lesser extent than the more stable CO2 and hydrocarbons, making them more available to react during a thermal event.
claims.quote: the reactive gases O2 and H2 are dissolved in the electrolyte to a lesser extent than the more stable CO2 and hydrocarbons. This means that the reactive gases are, to a larger extent, available to react with additional gases formed during a thermal event

claims.claim: Gas composition from aged LIB cells under abuse conditions has been shown to be different from that of fresh cells.
claims.quote: Gas composition from aged LIB cells under abuse conditions has been shown to be different from that of fresh cells, which indicates that there may be some 'chemical pre-processing' in an LIB cell during normal operation.

claims.claim: Slow thermal runaway initiation, such as homogeneous heating of the entire cell, favours higher relative amounts of CO2, whereas fast triggers like nail penetration or high-power localised heaters typically lead to incomplete oxidation and higher relative concentrations of CO.
claims.quote: Slow TR initiation, such as the homogeneous heating of the entire cell, favours higher relative amounts of CO2, whereas fast triggers, like nail penetration or high-power localised heaters, typically lead to incomplete oxidation and higher relative concentrations of CO.

claims.claim: Single-cell results cannot be extrapolated linearly to multi-cell configurations due to the higher temperatures realised in the latter case, which directly impacts chemical processing and rates of reaction.
claims.quote: single-cell results cannot be extrapolated linearly to multi-cell configurations due to the higher temperatures realised in the latter case, which directly impacts the chemical processing and rates of reaction.

claims.claim: For NCA cells studied by Golubkov et al., at low SOC CO2 dominated the vent gas, while CO increased and dominated at SOC greater than 60%, but the sum of CO and CO2 was consistent at about 70% for all cells.
claims.quote: An important trend was that at a low SOC, CO2 dominated, while CO increased and dominated at a SOC greater than 60%. However, the sum of the CO and CO2 was consistent at about 70% for all cells.

claims.claim: For LFP, NMC, and NCA LIBs, the relative concentration of inert CO2 decreases and the more reactive components including CO increase as SOC increases.
claims.quote: over the full range of SOCs, the relative concentration of inert CO2 decreases and the more reactive components, including CO, increase as SOC increases.

claims.claim: For highly reactive species such as HF and POF3, the presence of additional surfaces in multi-cell assemblies and batteries reduces actual gas concentrations due to rapid adsorption and reaction.
claims.quote: For highly reactive species, such as HF and POF3, the presence of additional surfaces in multi-cell assemblies and batteries reduces the actual gas concentrations due to rapid adsorption and reaction.

claims.claim: In NCA cells heated to 150 °C versus 160 °C, the additional 10 °C led to more than a doubling of gas volume, indicating significant internal processing occurred in the cell at this temperature.
claims.quote: there was quite a large difference between cells quenched at 150 ◦C and 160 ◦C; the additional 10 ◦C led to more than a doubling of the gas volume, indicating that significant internal processing occurred in the cell at this temperature.

claims.claim: O2 was measured in vent gases from a single LCO cell at 100% SOC but not from an array of five cells under similar conditions, with the authors suggesting shorter internal chemical processing time in the single cell as it cools faster.
claims.quote: A few percent of O2 was measured in vent gases from a single LCO cell at 100% SOC but not from an array of five cells in the same study under similar conditions. The authors suggest that the time frame for internal chemical processing is shorter in the single cell since it cools faster; therefore, O2 escape unreacted.

claims.claim: EMC shows signs of thermal decomposition at 175 °C when 1.2 M LiPF6 is present, while DMC is not affected by the salt at temperatures up to about 450 °C.
claims.quote: With 1.2 M of LiPF6, EMC shows signs of thermal decomposition already at 175 ◦C, while DMC is not affected.

claims.claim: LFP cathodes likely produce very little or no oxygen at all during thermal runaway, representing the lowest thermal runaway risk among cathode chemistries studied.
claims.quote: Lithium–iron phosphate (LFP) cathodes likely produce very little or no oxygen at all, which is represented by the half-cell (cathode) chemistry with the lowest risk going into TR.

claims.claim: The reactivity of oxygen-producing cathodes in terms of thermal runaway risk is ranked as LCO > NCA > NCM > LMO.
claims.quote: The reactivity of the oxygen-producing cathodes is ranked as follows: LCO > NCA > NCM > LMO

claims.claim: In overcharge-triggered venting experiments, the first vent occurred at or below 80 °C measured at the cell surface, whereas for temperature triggers, the first vent occurred at temperatures from 120 to 195 °C.
claims.quote: When overcharge was used to trigger TR, the venting occurred at or below 80 ◦C (measured at the surface of the cell). For temperature triggers, the first vent occurred at temperatures from 120 to 195 ◦C

claims.claim: The gas volume of the first vent was not dependent on SOC, based on the only study that varied SOC below 100%.
claims.quote: the only study with a lower SOC indicated that the gas volume of the first vent was not dependent on SOC

claims.claim: CO2 measurements from experiments where combustion occurred after gas release are not comparable to fresh vent gas CO2 measurements, and even without combustion, oxidation of CO to CO2 likely occurred in non-inert environments.
claims.quote: CO/CO2 from experiments where combustion occurred after gas release are not relevant to compare to fresh vent gases. However, even in cases where there was no combustion, the oxidation of CO to CO2 likely occurred in the presence of O2 and other gas-phase oxidisers if the environment was not inert.

claims.claim: O2 produced inside LIB cells is very unlikely to sustain a fire; LIB fires rely on atmospheric oxygen, though internally produced oxygen may be sufficient to feed low-intensity smouldering.
claims.quote: it can be concluded that it is very unlikely that LIB fires can be sustained by the internally released oxygen present in the gas mixture and that open flame fire relies on the availability of atmospheric oxygen. However, the oxygen produced by the cell may be sufficient to feed a low-intensity smouldering that may flare up if and when ambient oxygen from the atmosphere becomes available.

claims.claim: Rapid nail penetration triggering of LIB cells leads to thermal runaway on very short timescales with no preceding first vent.
claims.quote: Rapid penetration triggering leads to TR on very short timescales, with no preceding first vent

claims.claim: The boiling points of common carbonates at ambient conditions range from 91 °C for DMC to 248 °C for cyclic EC, with the order being DMC < EMC < DEC < EC.
claims.quote: The boiling points for the common carbonates at ambient conditions are in the range from 91 ◦C for the smallest carbonate DMC to 248 ◦C for the cyclic EC. The order of boiling points for four common carbonates are as follows: DMC < EMC < DEC < EC.

claims.claim: The decomposition of DEC occurs at a temperature approximately 100 K lower when Li-salt is present compared to without salt.
claims.quote: the decomposition of DEC occurs at a temperature of approximately 100 K lower with salt
